testingRequirements: Tests must validate user-facing behavior, not implementation details
Focus on what users actually do - integration tests over unit tests
Don't mock excessively - test real behavior where possible
Coverage metrics are meaningless - user flow coverage is everything

userStories.description:
## Context

**Branch**: All work for this epic is on one feature branch: `feat/cursor-agent-integration`. Do not create a separate branch for this ticket.
**Full plan**: Refer to the epic description for the complete plan, file manifest, technical reference, and context. Regenerate this ticket from the epic if requirements are unclear.
**Depends on**: CLI discovery ticket (findCursorAgentCli + isCursorAgentInstalled must exist)

## What

Create the launch script generator and TanStack server function for launching Cursor Agent CLI in a terminal.

## Files to modify

- `src/api/terminal.ts` — add `createCursorAgentLaunchScript()` and `launchCursorAgentInTerminal`

## Details

### `createCursorAgentLaunchScript(projectPath, context)`

Follow `createCopilotLaunchScript()` / `createOpenCodeLaunchScript()` pattern. Creates a bash script that:

1. `cd` to project path
2. Saves context to `.brain-dump-context.md` via heredoc (same unique delimiter pattern as other scripts)
3. Shows Brain Dump branding banner — amber color (`\033[0;33m`) matching existing Cursor branding
4. Verifies `agent` binary is available (or resolved path from `findCursorAgentCli`)
5. Launches: `agent --force --approve-mcps --trust -p "$(cat "$CONTEXT_FILE")"`
6. Handles non-zero exit codes with troubleshooting guidance
7. Cleans up context file
8. `exec bash` to keep terminal open

### `launchCursorAgentInTerminal` server function

`createServerFn({ method: "POST" })` — follow `launchCopilotInTerminal` pattern exactly:

1. Input: `{ ticketId, context, projectPath, preferredTerminal?, projectName, epicName, ticketTitle }`
2. Validate project path exists
3. Check `isCursorAgentInstalled()`
4. Detect/validate terminal emulator
5. Start ticket workflow (`startWorkflowForLaunch`)
6. Save current ticket state to `~/.brain-dump/current-ticket.json`
7. Create launch script via `createCursorAgentLaunchScript()`
8. Build terminal command with window title
9. Fire-and-forget launch via `exec()`

Returns `LaunchResult` with success/failure, method, message, terminal used, warnings.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `createCursorAgentLaunchScript()` generates valid bash with proper escaping
- [ ] `launchCursorAgentInTerminal` is exported as a `createServerFn`
- [ ] Server function starts ticket workflow before launching
- [ ] Server function saves ticket state for CLI tool
- [ ] Graceful handling when Cursor Agent CLI not installed
- [ ] Graceful handling when no terminal emulator found
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` passes
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes

userStories.description:
## Context

**Branch**: All work for this epic is on one feature branch: `feat/cursor-agent-integration`. Do not create a separate branch for this ticket.
**Full plan**: Refer to the epic description for the complete plan, file manifest, technical reference, and context. Regenerate this ticket from the epic if requirements are unclear.
**Depends on**: CLI discovery ticket + launch script ticket

## What

Make Cursor Agent a real Ralph AI backend so Ralph loops can run with Cursor Agent in headless mode.

## Files to modify

- `src/api/ralph-script.ts` — update `RalphAiBackend` type, add preflight check + AI invocation
- `src/api/ralph.ts` — update 3 ternary routing chains for `workingMethod === "cursor-agent"`

## Details

### ralph-script.ts

1. **Type**: Add `"cursor-agent"` to `RalphAiBackend` union: `"claude" | "opencode" | "codex" | "cursor-agent"`

2. **Preflight check** (in `generateRalphScript`): Verify CLI binary exists before entering loop:
   ```bash
   if ! command -v agent >/dev/null 2>&1; then
     echo "Cursor Agent CLI not found in PATH"
     echo "Install: curl https://cursor.com/install -fsS | bash"
     exit 1
   fi
   ```

3. **AI invocation**: The headless Ralph loop command:
   ```bash
   agent --force --approve-mcps --trust -p "$(cat "$PROMPT_FILE")"
   ```
   This mirrors Claude's `claude --dangerously-skip-permissions --output-format text -p "$(cat "$PROMPT_FILE")"`

4. **Display name**: `"Cursor Agent"` in the banner and log output (update `aiName` variable)

### ralph.ts

There are 3 ternary conditional chains that route by `workingMethod`. Each currently handles `"vscode"`, `"cursor"` (editor), and `"copilot-cli"`. Add `"cursor-agent"` case to each chain, routing to the new terminal launch function (not the editor launcher).

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `RalphAiBackend` type includes `"cursor-agent"`
- [ ] Ralph script preflight checks for Cursor Agent CLI binary
- [ ] Ralph loop uses `agent --force --approve-mcps --trust -p` for headless execution
- [ ] Banner displays "Cursor Agent" as the AI backend name
- [ ] All 3 routing chains in `ralph.ts` handle `"cursor-agent"` working method
- [ ] `pnpm type-check` passes
- [ ] `pnpm lint` passes

`~/.cursor/cli-config.json` created/merged with Brain Dump permission entries
Existing user permissions in cli-config.json preserved during merge

userStories.description:
## Context

**Branch**: All work for this epic is on one feature branch: `feat/cursor-agent-integration`. Do not create a separate branch for this ticket.
**Full plan**: Refer to the epic description for the complete plan, file manifest, technical reference, and context. Regenerate this ticket from the epic if requirements are unclear.

## What

Extend the existing `scripts/setup-cursor.sh` to also configure Cursor Agent CLI. One owner script for all `~/.cursor/` config.

## File to modify

- `scripts/setup-cursor.sh`

## Details

Add a new section after the existing MCP/agents/skills/commands steps:

### Step: Detect Cursor Agent CLI

Best-effort check for the binary:
1. Check `agent` in PATH, verify version output contains "cursor"
2. Fallback: check `~/.local/bin/agent`
3. Fallback: check `cursor-agent` in PATH
4. If not found: print install guidance (`curl https://cursor.com/install -fsS | bash`) but do NOT fail the script — editor setup must still succeed

### Step: Configure `~/.cursor/cli-config.json`

Brain Dump-managed permissions for approval-free Ralph. Cursor uses exact string matching:

```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": [
      "Shell(git)", "Shell(node)", "Shell(pnpm)", "Shell(npm)",
      "Shell(bash)", "Shell(sh)", "Shell(rg)", "Shell(find)",
      "Shell(ls)", "Shell(cat)", "Shell(sed)", "Shell(grep)",
      "Shell(mkdir)", "Shell(cp)", "Shell(mv)", "Shell(echo)",
      "Shell(test)", "Shell(which)"
    ],
    "deny": [
      "Shell(rm -rf /)", "Shell(sudo)", "Shell(shutdown)", "Shell(reboot)"
    ]
  }
}
```

**Merge strategy**: If `cli-config.json` already exists, read it, merge Brain Dump permission entries (preserving user entries), write back. Use the `node -e` JSON merge pattern already established for `mcp.json` in this same script.

### Step (optional): State enforcement hook

If Cursor hook behavior is confirmed, add `enforce-state-before-write.sh` to `~/.cursor/hooks/` with matching `hooks.json` config. Same logic as `setup-copilot-cli.sh` hook. Cursor hooks support `matcher` patterns (`"Write|Edit|Create"`).

If adding hooks, merge with existing `hooks.json` (don't overwrite).

### Step: Update summary output

Add Cursor Agent CLI status to the setup summary banner.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Script detects Cursor Agent CLI binary (best-effort, no failure on missing)
- [ ] `~/.cursor/cli-config.json` created/merged with Brain Dump permission entries
- [ ] Existing user permissions in cli-config.json preserved during merge
- [ ] Script is idempotent (safe to run multiple times)
- [ ] Summary output mentions Cursor Agent CLI status
- [ ] Existing Cursor editor setup (MCP, agents, skills, commands) is unchanged
- [ ] Script passes shellcheck or similar linting

userStories.description:
## Context

**Branch**: All work for this epic is on one feature branch: `feat/cursor-agent-integration`. Do not create a separate branch for this ticket.
**Full plan**: Refer to the epic description for the complete plan, file manifest, technical reference, and context. Regenerate this ticket from the epic if requirements are unclear.
**Depends on**: setup-cursor.sh extension ticket

## What

Update all install/uninstall entrypoints so Cursor Agent is covered everywhere Cursor is covered today.

## Files to modify

- `scripts/install.sh` — add Cursor Agent CLI detection
- `install.sh` (root) — add Cursor Agent CLI detection (if it delegates to scripts/install.sh, may only need the one)
- `scripts/uninstall.sh` — add Cursor Agent cleanup
- `uninstall.sh` (root) — add Cursor Agent cleanup

## Details

### Install

Add Cursor Agent CLI detection alongside existing Cursor editor detection:

```bash
# Detect Cursor Agent CLI
if command -v agent &>/dev/null; then
  if agent --version 2>&1 | grep -qi "cursor"; then
    CURSOR_AGENT_AVAILABLE=1
    echo -e "${GREEN}✓${NC} Cursor Agent CLI detected"
  else
    echo -e "${YELLOW}○${NC} 'agent' found but not Cursor Agent CLI"
  fi
else
  echo -e "${YELLOW}○${NC} Cursor Agent CLI not found"
fi
```

The `--cursor` flag and `--all` both trigger the extended `setup-cursor.sh` which now handles both editor and CLI. No new flag needed.

### Uninstall

Cursor Agent cleanup removes only Brain Dump-managed config:
- Remove Brain Dump permission entries from `~/.cursor/cli-config.json` (merge-remove, not delete)
- Remove hooks if installed (`~/.cursor/hooks/enforce-state-before-write.sh`, clean hooks.json)
- Do NOT remove MCP, agents, skills, commands (shared with editor, handled by existing Cursor uninstall path)
- Do NOT remove the `agent` binary or Cursor app

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] `--cursor` installs both editor and CLI config
- [ ] `--all` includes Cursor Agent
- [ ] Detection distinguishes Cursor's `agent` from other binaries
- [ ] Uninstall removes only Brain Dump-managed Cursor Agent config
- [ ] Uninstall preserves MCP/agents/skills/commands (shared with editor)
- [ ] Uninstall does not remove `agent` binary
- [ ] Scripts are idempotent

userStories.description:
## Context

**Branch**: All work for this epic is on one feature branch: `feat/cursor-agent-integration`. Do not create a separate branch for this ticket.
**Full plan**: Refer to the epic description for the complete plan, file manifest, technical reference, and context. Regenerate this ticket from the epic if requirements are unclear.
**Depends on**: All implementation tickets should be complete before docs are finalized.

## What

Update documentation to clearly separate Cursor Editor and Cursor Agent CLI integrations.

## Areas to update

### 1. Cursor setup docs

Clearly separate:
- Cursor Editor integration (opens GUI, context file, `cursor` CLI or `open -a Cursor`)
- Cursor Agent CLI integration (terminal, headless, Ralph via `agent` binary)
- What `--cursor` now installs (both editor and CLI config)
- How Ralph behaves in each mode (editor = opens Cursor with context file; agent = headless terminal loop)

### 2. README environment matrix

Add Cursor Agent as a distinct row alongside Cursor Editor in the environment support table. The table currently has columns for AI Review, Hook Enforcement, Telemetry. Cursor Agent should show:
- AI Review: Full
- Hook Enforcement: Optional (hooks.json if configured)
- Telemetry: MCP self-instrumentation

### 3. CLAUDE.md environment table

Update the Multi-Environment Support table to include Cursor Agent as a separate entry from Cursor.

### 4. Troubleshooting notes

Add troubleshooting guidance for:
- CLI install and detection (`agent` vs `cursor-agent` binary name ambiguity)
- MCP visibility (`agent mcp list` should show brain-dump)
- Permission config (`~/.cursor/cli-config.json` format, exact `"Shell()"` string matching)
- Hook enforcement if enabled (state file location, valid states)

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] Cursor Editor and Cursor Agent CLI are clearly distinguished in all docs
- [ ] README environment matrix includes Cursor Agent row
- [ ] CLAUDE.md environment table includes Cursor Agent
- [ ] Setup docs explain what `--cursor` installs
- [ ] Troubleshooting section covers CLI detection and permissions
- [ ] No broken links or stale references

userStories.description:
## Context

**Branch**: All work for this epic is on one feature branch: `feat/cursor-agent-integration`. Do not create a separate branch for this ticket.
**Full plan**: Refer to the epic description for the complete plan, file manifest, technical reference, and context. Regenerate this ticket from the epic if requirements are unclear.
**Depends on**: All implementation tickets should be complete before test coverage is finalized.

## What

Add test mocks for the new server function, backend unit tests for discovery/launch/Ralph generation, and script test coverage.

## Test areas

### 1. UI test mocks

Every test file that mocks `launchCursorInTerminal` also needs `launchCursorAgentInTerminal: vi.fn()`:

- `src/routes/-ticket.$id.telemetry.test.tsx`
- `src/routes/-epic.$id.test.tsx`
- `src/routes/-ticket.$id.test.tsx`
- `src/components/TicketModal.test.tsx`
- `src/components/TicketModal.telemetry.test.tsx`
- `src/components/tickets/EditTicketModal.launch.test.tsx`

### 2. Backend unit tests

Tests for the new functions (follow existing test patterns in the codebase):

- `findCursorAgentCli()` — returns path when installed, null when not, distinguishes from non-Cursor `agent` binaries
- `isCursorAgentInstalled()` — returns structured InstallCheck
- `createCursorAgentLaunchScript()` — generates valid bash script with proper escaping
- Ralph script generation with `"cursor-agent"` backend — correct preflight check, correct invocation command
- Working method validation — `"cursor-agent"` accepted

### 3. Script test coverage

**File**: `scripts/install-scripts.test.ts`

- `--cursor` and `--all` install parity (both trigger Cursor Agent setup)
- Uninstall parity (removes Brain Dump config only)
- `mcp.json` merge/remove behavior
- `cli-config.json` merge/remove behavior (new)

### Testing philosophy reminder

Per CLAUDE.md: test user-facing behavior, not implementation details. For backend functions, the "user" is the calling code — test inputs and outputs, not internal mechanics.

## Acceptance criteria

- [ ] All 6 test files have `launchCursorAgentInTerminal` mock
- [ ] `pnpm test` passes with all new mocks
- [ ] Backend discovery/launch functions have test coverage
- [ ] Script install/uninstall tests cover Cursor Agent config
- [ ] No tests for implementation details (only inputs/outputs)

techStack: Database: SQLite with better-sqlite3, Drizzle ORM
State: TanStack Query for server state

dos: Use Drizzle ORM: `db.select().from(tickets)`
Use typed schema imports: `import { tickets } from "../lib/schema"`
Use `eq()`, `and()`, `sql` from drizzle-orm for conditions
Use transactions for multi-table operations: `db.transaction(() => {...})`
Use `.get()` for single row, `.all()` for multiple
donts: Raw SQL strings: `db.run("SELECT * FROM tickets")`
String-based table names
String concatenation for WHERE clauses
Multiple independent queries that should be atomic
Assume query returns what you expect without checking

dos: Use `useQuery` with `queryKeys` for data fetching
Use `useMutation` with `onSuccess` for state changes
Invalidate queries after mutations: `queryClient.invalidateQueries({ queryKey: queryKeys.tickets })`
Use centralized `queryKeys` object from `src/lib/hooks.ts`
Create custom hooks in `src/lib/hooks.ts` for reusable query logic
donts: `useState` + `useEffect` for fetched data
Direct API calls without proper cache invalidation
Manual cache manipulation or refetch after every change
Hardcoded query key strings throughout components
Duplicate query setup in multiple components

dos: Use Tailwind classes for static styles: `className="p-4 bg-slate-800"`
Use CSS variables for theming: `var(--bg-primary)`, `var(--shadow-modal)`
Reserve inline styles for dynamic/computed values: `style={{ width: `${percent}%` }}`
Define shared style constants at module level for referential stability
Use `React.CSSProperties` type for inline style objects when needed
donts: Inline style objects for single-use styles
Hardcoded colors that don't adapt to themes
Mix Tailwind and inline styles inconsistently
Create new objects inside components that are reused
Untyped style objects that miss IDE autocomplete

dos: Use `createServerFn({ method: "GET" })` for reads, `"POST"` for writes
Use `.inputValidator()` for type-safe input handling
Return typed data directly: `return db.select().from(tickets).all()`
Use `ensureExists()` for required lookups: `ensureExists(project, "Project")`
Import from `@tanstack/react-start` (not `@tanstack/react-start/server`)
donts: Mix GET/POST inconsistently
Access raw input without validation
Wrap in unnecessary response objects
Return null and let caller handle missing data
Wrong import path

dos: Use Zod schemas for input validation: `{ ticketId: z.string() }`
Return structured `{ content: [{ type: "text", text: ... }] }` format
Set `isError: true` for error responses
Use `log.info()` / `log.error()` from `../lib/logging.js`
Include helpful error messages: `"Project not found. Use list_projects to see available."`
donts: Trust input without validation
Return plain strings
Return error text without the error flag
`console.log` in MCP server code
Generic "Not found" errors

dos: Test user behavior: what users see, click, and experience
Integration tests for workflows: test components together
Real database fixtures with actual schema
Tests that fail when user-facing behavior breaks
Ask: "Does this test catch bugs users would encounter?"
donts: Test implementation details, internal state, or private methods
Unit tests for every function
Excessive mocking of internals
Tests that break on refactoring internals
Chase 100% code coverage as a goal

verificationSteps: Run `pnpm type-check` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm lint` - must pass with no errors
Run `pnpm test` - all tests must pass
Added tests for new functionality (ONLY tests that verify real user behavior - see Testing Philosophy above)
Used typed error classes (not generic `Error`)
Used Drizzle ORM (not raw SQL) - see DO/DON'T table above
Followed existing patterns from DO/DON'T tables
No hardcoded values that should be configurable
Existing tests still pass
No regressions in related functionality
Updated tests if behavior changed
Did not break backward compatibility (unless explicitly requested)
Manually verified in browser at `localhost:4242`
Checked responsive layout
Verified TanStack Query invalidates and updates correctly
Accessibility: keyboard navigation works, proper ARIA labels
Migration file created via `pnpm db:generate`
Migration tested with `pnpm db:migrate`
Backup tested if schema changed (use `pnpm brain-dump backup` then test restore)
Updated `src/lib/schema.ts` with proper types and constraints
Tested tool via Claude Code integration
Verified error responses are informative (see DO/DON'T table)
Updated tool documentation if interface changed
Added Zod schema for input validation
All acceptance criteria from ticket met
Work summary added via `comment` tool, `action: "add"` (for Ralph sessions)
Session completed with appropriate outcome (for Ralph sessions)
Committed with proper message format: `feat(<ticket-id>): <description>`

epicTitle: Add Cursor Agent CLI as a First-Class Brain Dump Environment
epicDescription:
# Add Cursor Agent CLI as a First-Class Brain Dump Environment

## Summary

- Extend Brain Dump's existing Cursor integration so `--cursor` installs and manages both:
  - **Cursor Editor** for GUI/editor launches
  - **Cursor Agent** for terminal/CLI launches
- Introduce `cursor-agent` as a distinct Brain Dump provider/working method instead of overloading the current cursor editor path.
- Support both:
  - Interactive terminal launches with Cursor Agent
  - Approval-free Ralph automation with Cursor Agent headless mode
- Keep v1 focused on native Brain Dump integration, not Cursor plugin packaging.

---

## Context

Brain Dump supports 6 AI coding environments today. The existing "Cursor" integration opens the Cursor editor (GUI) with a context file. Cursor also ships a standalone terminal CLI that supports headless mode, auto-approve, multi-model access (GPT-5.x, Gemini 3.x, Claude, etc.), full MCP integration, and hooks. Adding this CLI as a distinct "Cursor Agent" environment gives Brain Dump a multi-model Ralph backend without requiring the Cursor editor.

**Key distinction**: `cursor` = open Cursor editor GUI. `cursor-agent` = run the Cursor CLI in a terminal. These are different execution models and remain separate launch targets.

**Binary name**: The official Cursor CLI docs (cursor.com/docs/cli/installation, cursor.com/docs/cli/reference/parameters) use `agent` as the binary name, installed to `~/.local/bin/agent`. Discovery should check for `agent` first, with a fallback check for `cursor-agent` if version detection fails. Verify via version output that it's Cursor's binary.

---

## 1. Setup and Installation

### 1.1 Extend `scripts/setup-cursor.sh`

Single owner script for all `~/.cursor/` config. Add a new section after existing MCP/agents/skills/commands setup:

**Cursor Agent CLI detection**: Best-effort check for the binary. If not found, print install guidance (`curl https://cursor.com/install -fsS | bash`). Do not fail the script -- editor setup should still succeed.

**`~/.cursor/cli-config.json`**: Brain Dump-managed permissions for approval-free Ralph. Cursor uses exact string matching for permissions:
```json
{
  "permissions": {
    "allow": [
      "Shell(git)", "Shell(node)", "Shell(pnpm)", "Shell(npm)",
      "Shell(bash)", "Shell(sh)", "Shell(rg)", "Shell(find)",
      "Shell(ls)", "Shell(cat)", "Shell(sed)", "Shell(grep)",
      "Shell(mkdir)", "Shell(cp)", "Shell(mv)", "Shell(echo)",
      "Shell(test)", "Shell(which)"
    ],
    "deny": [
      "Shell(rm -rf /)", "Shell(sudo)", "Shell(shutdown)", "Shell(reboot)"
    ]
  }
}
```
Merge strategy: read existing config, add Brain Dump entries, preserve user entries. Use the `node -e` JSON merge pattern already established for `mcp.json` in the same script.

**Optional hook** (hardening, not foundation): If Cursor hook behavior is confirmed in practice, add `enforce-state-before-write.sh` to `~/.cursor/hooks/` with `hooks.json` config. Blocks Write/Edit/Create unless Ralph state is implementing/testing/committing. Cursor hooks support `matcher` patterns (`"Write|Edit|Create"`) for tool filtering. Same logic as the Copilot CLI hook already in `setup-copilot-cli.sh`.

### 1.2 Update install entrypoints

**Files**: `scripts/install.sh`, `install.sh` (root)

Add Cursor Agent CLI detection alongside existing Cursor editor detection. The `--cursor` flag and `--all` both trigger the extended `setup-cursor.sh` which now handles both editor and CLI.

Detection must distinguish `agent` binary as Cursor's (generic name problem):
```bash
if command -v agent &>/dev/null; then
  if agent --version 2>&1 | grep -qi "cursor"; then
    CURSOR_AGENT_AVAILABLE=1
  fi
fi
```

### 1.3 Update uninstall entrypoints

**Files**: `scripts/uninstall.sh`, `uninstall.sh` (root)

Cursor Agent cleanup removes only Brain Dump-managed config:
- Remove Brain Dump permission entries from `~/.cursor/cli-config.json`
- Remove hooks if installed (`~/.cursor/hooks/enforce-state-before-write.sh`, update `hooks.json`)
- Do NOT remove MCP, agents, skills, commands (shared with editor)
- Do NOT remove the `agent` binary or Cursor app

### 1.4 Update `scripts/install-scripts.test.ts`

Add test coverage for `--cursor` installing both editor and CLI config, `cli-config.json` merge/remove, and uninstall parity.

---

## 2. Backend: CLI Discovery, Launch, Ralph Backend

### 2.1 CLI discovery

**File**: `src/api/ralph-launchers.ts`

Add `findCursorAgentCli(): Promise<string | null>`. Pattern: follow `findCursorCli()`. Check `agent` in PATH and verify via version output, fallback to `~/.local/bin/agent`, fallback to `cursor-agent`. Use `execFileNoThrow` from `src/utils/execFileNoThrow.ts` per project security rules.

### 2.2 Install check

**File**: `src/api/terminal.ts`

Add `isCursorAgentInstalled(): Promise<InstallCheck>`. Pattern: follow `isCopilotInstalled()`. Calls `findCursorAgentCli()` and returns structured result with install guidance on failure.

### 2.3 Launch script

**File**: `src/api/terminal.ts`

Add `createCursorAgentLaunchScript(projectPath, context): Promise<string>`. Pattern: follow `createCopilotLaunchScript()` / `createOpenCodeLaunchScript()`.

Creates a bash script that:
- `cd` to project, save context to `.brain-dump-context.md`
- Banner with amber branding (matching existing Cursor color)
- Verify CLI binary available
- Launch: `agent --force --approve-mcps --trust -p "$(cat "$CONTEXT_FILE")"`
- Handle exit codes with guidance
- Cleanup context file, `exec bash`

### 2.4 Server function

**File**: `src/api/terminal.ts`

Add `launchCursorAgentInTerminal` as a `createServerFn({ method: "POST" })`. Pattern: follow `launchCopilotInTerminal` exactly (validate path, check install, detect terminal, start workflow, save state, create script, build terminal command, fire-and-forget launch).

### 2.5 Ralph AI backend

**File**: `src/api/ralph-script.ts`

- Update type: add `"cursor-agent"` to `RalphAiBackend` union
- Add preflight check: verify CLI binary exists
- Add AI invocation for Ralph loop: `agent --force --approve-mcps --trust -p "$(cat "$PROMPT_FILE")"`
- Add display name: `"Cursor Agent"`

### 2.6 Ralph routing

**File**: `src/api/ralph.ts`

There are 3 ternary conditional chains that route by `workingMethod`. Each currently handles `"vscode"`, `"cursor"`, and `"copilot-cli"`. Add `"cursor-agent"` case to each chain, routing to the new terminal launch function.

---

## 3. UI: Launch Options and Handlers

### 3.1 Rename existing labels

**File**: `src/components/tickets/LaunchActions.tsx`

- `"cursor"` entry: name `"Cursor"` -> `"Cursor Editor"`
- `"ralph-cursor"` entry: name `"Ralph (Cursor)"` -> `"Ralph (Cursor Editor)"`

### 3.2 Add new launch types

**File**: `src/components/tickets/LaunchActions.tsx`

Add to `LaunchType` union: `"cursor-agent"` | `"ralph-cursor-agent"`

Add to `LAUNCH_OPTIONS` array:
- `"cursor-agent"`: name "Cursor Agent", description "Terminal AI with multi-model", icon `Terminal` (not `Monitor` -- this is CLI, not GUI), amber color
- `"ralph-cursor-agent"`: name "Ralph (Cursor Agent)", description "Autonomous multi-model agent", icon `Bot`, amber color

### 3.3 Add handler cases to all launch surfaces

Every component that handles launch types needs `"cursor-agent"` and `"ralph-cursor-agent"` cases calling `launchCursorAgentInTerminal`. Files and their patterns:

| File | Pattern | What exists to follow |
|------|---------|----------------------|
| `src/components/tickets/EditTicketModal.tsx` | If/else chain | `cursor` / `ralph-cursor` cases |
| `src/components/TicketModal.tsx` | If/else chain | `cursor` case |
| `src/components/EpicModal.tsx` | Switch + launch menu | `cursor` case |
| `src/components/epics/EpicDetailHeader.tsx` | Switch + UI arrays + review provider options | `cursor` case + `workingMethodOverride: "cursor"` |
| `src/routes/ticket.$id.tsx` | If/else chain | `cursor` case |

Each file also needs `launchCursorAgentInTerminal` added to imports from `src/api/terminal.ts`.

### 3.4 Do NOT add to editor-only surfaces

`src/api/dev-tools.ts` has an `EditorInfo.name` type (`"vscode" | "cursor" | "vim" | "neovim"`) and "Open in Editor" functionality. Cursor Agent is NOT an editor -- do not add it here.

---

## 4. Working Method, Schema, Provider Bookkeeping

### 4.1 Type/schema updates

Every file that enumerates working methods needs `"cursor-agent"` added:

| File | What to update |
|------|----------------|
| `src/components/projects/WorkingMethodSelect.tsx` | `WorkingMethod` type union + `WORKING_METHOD_OPTIONS` array (add entry with icon, label, description) |
| `src/components/settings/settings-form-schema.ts` | `workingMethodSchema` Zod enum |
| `mcp-server/types.ts` | `DbProject.workingMethod` union + `DbSettings.defaultWorkingMethod` union |
| `mcp-server/tools/admin.ts` | `WORKING_METHODS` array/Zod enum constant |
| `src/lib/schema.ts` | Working method field type if defined here |

No DB migration needed -- `defaultWorkingMethod` is stored as text.

### 4.2 Comment authors / analytics

**File**: `src/lib/comment-authors.ts`

Add `"cursor-agent"` to:
- `BASE_COMMENT_AUTHORS` array
- `COMMENT_AUTHOR_STYLES` record (amber color to match branding)
- `COMMENT_AUTHOR_LABELS` record (label: "Cursor Agent")

This ensures Cursor Agent work is attributed correctly in ticket activity logs, not bucketed as generic user activity.

### 4.3 Environment detection

**Files**: `src/lib/environment.ts`, `mcp-server/lib/environment.ts`

These already detect the Cursor editor environment via `CURSOR` env flag. Add detection for Cursor Agent CLI environment. The setup script sets `CURSOR_AGENT: "1"` env var in MCP config -- add a corresponding detection function (`hasCursorAgentEnvironment()` / `isCursorAgent()`).

Keep existing `isCursor()` for editor detection.

### 4.4 Doctor / verification

**File**: `cli/commands/admin.ts` (or wherever `brain-dump doctor` is implemented)

Add Cursor Agent CLI status to doctor output:
- Binary found / not found (and which name)
- Version
- MCP server configured in `~/.cursor/mcp.json`
- `cli-config.json` permissions configured
- Optional: hook installed

---

## 5. Documentation

### 5.1 Update Cursor setup docs

Clearly separate:
- Cursor Editor integration (opens GUI, context file)
- Cursor Agent CLI integration (terminal, headless, Ralph)
- What `--cursor` installs now (both)
- How Ralph behaves in each mode

### 5.2 Update README environment matrix

Add Cursor Agent as a distinct row alongside Cursor Editor in the environment support table.

### 5.3 Troubleshooting notes

- CLI install and detection (`agent` vs `cursor-agent` ambiguity)
- MCP visibility (`agent mcp list` should show brain-dump)
- Permission config (`cli-config.json` format)
- Hook enforcement if enabled

---

## 6. Tests

### 6.1 Script tests

**File**: `scripts/install-scripts.test.ts`

- `--cursor` and `--all` install parity
- Uninstall parity
- `mcp.json` merge/remove
- `cli-config.json` merge/remove

### 6.2 Backend unit tests

- `findCursorAgentCli()` discovery logic
- `isCursorAgentInstalled()` check
- `createCursorAgentLaunchScript()` output
- Ralph script generation with `"cursor-agent"` backend
- Working method validation

### 6.3 UI test mocks

Every test file that mocks `launchCursorInTerminal` also needs `launchCursorAgentInTerminal`:

- `src/routes/-ticket.$id.telemetry.test.tsx`
- `src/routes/-epic.$id.test.tsx`
- `src/routes/-ticket.$id.test.tsx`
- `src/components/TicketModal.test.tsx`
- `src/components/TicketModal.telemetry.test.tsx`
- `src/components/tickets/EditTicketModal.launch.test.tsx`

### 6.4 Manual verification

1. Run Cursor setup -- verify both editor and CLI config installed
2. Verify CLI binary detected
3. Verify MCP visible from CLI
4. Open Brain Dump UI -- "Cursor Editor" and "Cursor Agent" both appear
5. Interactive Cursor Agent launch from ticket -- terminal opens with context
6. Ralph (Cursor Agent) launch for ticket and epic -- headless loop runs
7. Verify no approval prompts during Ralph
8. Verify doctor output shows Cursor Agent status
9. Uninstall -- only Brain Dump config removed

---

## Follow-Up Debt (Not V1)

- **Provider registry consolidation**: Duplicated launch branching across 6+ components should become a shared registry. Deferred unless duplication becomes a blocker during implementation.
- **Cursor plugin/marketplace packaging**: V1 is native Brain Dump integration only.
- **Cursor cloud agents**: V1 uses local CLI only.
- **Analytics audit for all newer providers**: Cursor Agent should be included in any future analytics cleanup.

---

## Assumptions

- V1 is native Brain Dump integration only; no Cursor marketplace/plugin packaging.
- `agent` is the primary CLI command unless local verification proves a supported alias should also be handled.
- Cursor Agent CLI will reuse Cursor's MCP and rules configuration; skills/subagents are not required for the Ralph loop in v1.
- Provider-registry refactoring is useful but deferred unless implementation duplication becomes a blocker.
- No DB migration required for working method values because the field is stored as text.

---

## Complete File Manifest

| File | Change |
|------|--------|
| `scripts/setup-cursor.sh` | Extend: CLI detection, cli-config.json, optional hook |
| `scripts/install.sh` | Add Cursor Agent detection |
| `install.sh` (root) | Add Cursor Agent detection |
| `scripts/uninstall.sh` | Add Cursor Agent cleanup |
| `uninstall.sh` (root) | Add Cursor Agent cleanup |
| `scripts/install-scripts.test.ts` | Add test coverage |
| `src/api/ralph-launchers.ts` | Add `findCursorAgentCli()` |
| `src/api/terminal.ts` | Add `isCursorAgentInstalled()`, `createCursorAgentLaunchScript()`, `launchCursorAgentInTerminal` |
| `src/api/ralph-script.ts` | Add `"cursor-agent"` to `RalphAiBackend`, preflight, invocation |
| `src/api/ralph.ts` | Update 3 ternary routing chains |
| `src/components/tickets/LaunchActions.tsx` | Rename labels, add types + options |
| `src/components/tickets/EditTicketModal.tsx` | Add handler cases + import |
| `src/components/TicketModal.tsx` | Add handler cases + import |
| `src/components/EpicModal.tsx` | Add handler cases + import |
| `src/components/epics/EpicDetailHeader.tsx` | Add handler cases + review provider + import |
| `src/routes/ticket.$id.tsx` | Add handler cases + import |
| `src/components/projects/WorkingMethodSelect.tsx` | Add to type + options array |
| `src/components/settings/settings-form-schema.ts` | Update Zod enum |
| `mcp-server/types.ts` | Update 2 union types |
| `mcp-server/tools/admin.ts` | Update WORKING_METHODS enum |
| `src/lib/schema.ts` | Update working method type |
| `src/lib/comment-authors.ts` | Add to 3 structures |
| `src/lib/environment.ts` | Add Cursor Agent detection |
| `mcp-server/lib/environment.ts` | Add Cursor Agent detection |
| `cli/commands/admin.ts` | Update doctor output |
| 6 test files | Add `launchCursorAgentInTerminal` mock |

---

## Cursor Agent CLI -- Technical Reference

| Property | Value |
|----------|-------|
| Binary name | `agent` (verify via `agent --version` contains "cursor") |
| Install | `curl https://cursor.com/install -fsS \| bash` |
| Default location | `~/.local/bin/agent` |
| Headless flag | `-p` or `--print` |
| Auto-approve files+shell | `--force` or `--yolo` |
| Auto-approve MCP | `--approve-mcps` |
| Skip trust prompt | `--trust` |
| Model selection | `--model <name>` (e.g., `gpt-5.2`, `claude-opus-4-6`) |
| Output formats | `text` (default), `json`, `stream-json` |
| Global config | `~/.cursor/cli-config.json` |
| Project config | `<project>/.cursor/cli.json` (permissions only) |
| MCP config | `~/.cursor/mcp.json` (shared with editor) |
| Hooks config | `~/.cursor/hooks.json` (shared with editor) |
| Rules | `.cursor/rules/`, `AGENTS.md`, `CLAUDE.md` |
| Skills | `.cursor/skills/`, `~/.cursor/skills/` |
| Subagents | `.cursor/agents/`, `~/.cursor/agents/` |
| Auth | `CURSOR_API_KEY` env var or `agent login` |
| Permissions format | `"Shell(command)"` exact string matching |

### Ralph Loop Command

```bash
agent --force --approve-mcps --trust -p "$(cat "$PROMPT_FILE")"
```
